description: Rubric sensitivity analysis: measures how scoring TYPE affects inter-judge agreement. Controlled PEMAT pair (rubric1 BINARY vs rubric5 LIKERT) isolates scale effect. 50 questions x 5 rubrics x 3 variants = 750 rows (~46 min on HPC).

controlled_pair.what_is_held_constant: All 5 PEMAT item criteria are identical word-for-word
controlled_pair.research_question: Does a BINARY scorer produce higher inter-judge agreement than a LIKERT scorer on identical medical evaluation criteria?

cross_rubric_variants.description: All 5 rubrics re-run under 3 forced scoring variants to test scoring sensitivity across different rubric types
